seoTitle: Rennie vs Renwick — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rennie and Renwick. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rennie and Renwick.
heading: Rennie vs Renwick — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rennie, New South Wales and Renwick, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rennie has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Renwick (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rennie or Renwick safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rennie has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Renwick scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rennie scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Renwick scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rennie or Renwick?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rennie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
